reviewer: new-demo build wave (Python + in-browser runnability proof, headless Chrome real download+inference on index + all 4 ladder pages, cached-reload auto-init probe, mobile+desktop overflow check, light/dark screenshots)

rubric.evidence: REAL in-browser inference verified two ways before building: (1) Python onnxruntime on en_PP-OCRv3_det_infer.onnx — [1,1,H,W] probability map whose high-prob pixels land exactly on rendered text (bbox x42-373,y63-329 for a 3-line test image). (2) In-browser ORT-web WASM harness (onnxruntime-web@1.21.0, dynamic import) — session in='x' out='sigmoid_0.tmp_0', 105 ms, prob>0.3 pixels covering all text. transformers.js@3.7.5 has NO DBNet text-detection class and no text-detection pipeline task, so the demo runs the ONNX graph directly via raw ONNX Runtime Web in a Web Worker (isolated per-worker pin, like face-embedding/yolov10). Headless build verify: fresh profile -> Download (~2.4 MB) -> ready -> Detect produced 5 region boxes on the cafe sign (inference 128 ms, decode 13 ms), 0 console errors, 0 net failures.
rubric.notes: Model breezedeus/cnstd-ppocr-en_PP-OCRv3_det (Apache-2.0), NCHW [1,3,H,W] ImageNet-normalised input -> [1,1,H,W] probability map; the DB decode (threshold -> connected components -> min-area oriented box -> unclip) is in the worker.

rubric.evidence: Output shape + semantic sanity confirmed on every page (boxes drawn on the image, counts match content): index/basics found 5 regions on the cafe sign; practical detected + redacted 19 regions on the receipt (every line item + price, verified visually); wild recovered oriented-box angles of 12/18/-8/-18 deg on the rotated poster; multi-model detected 5 regions and TrOCR read them ('THE CORNER CAFE','FRESH COFFEE & PASTRIES' exact). The raw probability map is shown as an indigo heatmap in 'See inside', with a per-region confidence + angle + size list.
rubric.notes: object-detection task template ('bounding boxes with class labels + confidence drawn on the image') fits: here the single class is 'text region', with per-region confidence + recovered angle.

rubric.evidence: conformance declares-model-id=pass (breezedeus/cnstd-ppocr-en_PP-OCRv3_det linked to its card), declares-task=pass (object-detection), declares-backend=pass (WASM), declares-quantisation=pass (fp32), inference-off-main-thread=pass (new Worker + worker.js). At-a-glance names the exact model, runtime (onnxruntime-web 1.21), NCHW input / probability-map output shapes, and honestly notes transformers.js has no DBNet class — no invented API.

conformance shared-auto-init-loader=pass — every page loads through createModelLoader. The worker fetches the ONNX THROUGH Cache Storage (ppocr-det-onnx-cache) with a content-length Response so lib/model-cache.js scans '/breezedeus/cnstd-ppocr-en_PP-OCRv3_det/' and the shared loader auto-inits on a returning visit (no Download button), plus the per-model clear-cache control works. Cached-reload probe confirmed the loader shows 'Model ready' + Clear cached model on the second navigation.

conformance loads-clean=pass (0 console errors on load across index + 4 ladder pages), live-status-region=pass (role=status aria-live), errors-on-the-page=pass. Download progress streams from the worker (content-length) into the loader progress bar; failures surface in the .status.err line and the loader offers Retry. Fresh-profile load shows the honest Download state.
rubric.notes: runs-without-webgpu-fallback: WASM-only (requiresWebGPU:false) — runs everywhere, verified on the no-GPU headless runner.

rubric.evidence: Every control exercised headless and is labelled + keyboard-operable: sample thumbs (role=button, tabindex, Enter/Space), dropzones + file inputs, Detect / Redact / Download-redacted / Detect&read buttons, and the region-confidence + detection-resolution <input type=range> sliders. buttons-have-names=pass, images-have-alt=pass. The practical Download-redacted button exports the in-page canvas — no upload.
rubric.notes: Sliders re-run detection with new params (a deliberate, bounded single forward pass off-thread).

rubric.evidence: All boxes, the heatmap, and the counts come from the on-device ORT-web run — no canned values. Distinct, correct results per image (5 sign / 19 receipt / 4 poster with real angles) prove the output tracks the real probability map, not a placeholder.

conformance images-have-alt=pass, buttons-have-names=pass, live-status-region=pass. Sample images have descriptive alt; the detection/heatmap canvases carry role=img + aria-label updated with the region count; the decoded-region list is a real <ol> so a screen reader gets the count + per-region confidence/angle as text (the accessible fallback for canvas content per the expose-canvas-content guide). Status uses role=status aria-live=polite.
rubric.notes: Sample images are announced as generated-for-this-demo (no real people/brands).

rubric.evidence: conformance responsive-desktop=pass, responsive-mobile=pass. Measured mobile (360x740 DPR3) horizontal overflow = 0 px on index + all 4 ladder pages; desktop 1280x800 screenshots reviewed — the two-column (image | controls / redacted / read) layout uses flex:1 1 300px + min-inline-size:0 and stacks on mobile; canvases are inline-size:100% block-size:auto; no clipped controls. Dark + light both legible.
rubric.notes: aspect-ratio-free canvases sized from the image; no 100vh traps.

rubric.evidence: Inference AND the full DB decode (threshold, connected-components labeling, convex hull + rotating-calipers min-area rect, unclip) run in a Web Worker; the probability heatmap is transferred back as a Uint8 buffer (zero-copy) and the main thread only draws boxes/heatmap. Inference ~128 ms + decode ~13 ms on the headless runner — no main-thread long task. The dense-output composite hotspot is avoided by keeping the pixel work in the worker.
rubric.notes: Guidance applied: break-up-long-tasks / identify-inp-causes (inference + dense pixel decode off the main thread; transfer not clone).

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive image + controls two column no horizontal overflow on mobile flexbox
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flexbox with flex:1 1 <basis> and set min-inline-size:0 on flex items with wide content so they shrink instead of overflowing; use gap + flex-wrap; size media with inline-size:100% block-size:auto.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: Applied — the image/controls and original/redacted columns use flex:1 1 300px with min-inline-size:0 and stack on narrow viewports; canvases are inline-size:100% block-size:auto. Verified 0 px horizontal overflow at 360x740 on all pages.

guidanceConsulted.query: make canvas bounding-box detection output accessible to screen readers
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Regular canvas content is invisible to assistive tech; provide an accessible representation (role=img + aria-label, and a real text list of the content).
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: Applied (fallback strategy) — HTML-in-canvas (requestPaint) is not Baseline, so instead each detection/heatmap canvas has role=img + a count-bearing aria-label, and the decoded regions are rendered as a real <ol> list (confidence/angle/size per region) that screen readers and find-in-page can reach.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low move image inference and pixel post-processing off main thread web worker
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move long main-thread JavaScript (inference + dense pixel loops) off the main thread into a worker; transfer buffers instead of cloning.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: Applied — ONNX inference and the entire connected-components + min-area-rect decode run in a module worker; the probability heatmap is transferred back as a Uint8 buffer, and the main thread only draws. No blocking loop during interaction.

openQuestions: DBNet gives a per-pixel probability map but not reading order/line grouping; a follow-up could add a simple line-merge heuristic before TrOCR for multi-line blocks.
A WebGPU ORT-web EP path could speed detection on very large images — worth a progressive-enhancement follow-up once verifiable on a GPU runner.

followUpGoals.goal: Add a live-camera ladder demo (lib/capture-ux.js, user-initiated) that highlights text regions in a webcam frame for assistive capture.
Add a full detect -> deskew (using the recovered angle) -> TrOCR pipeline so rotated lines are straightened before reading.

summary: New demo: on-device scene TEXT DETECTION (locate, not read) via PaddleOCR PP-OCRv3 DBNet (breezedeus/cnstd-ppocr-en_PP-OCRv3_det, Apache-2.0) run through raw ONNX Runtime Web in a worker, with the full DB decode (threshold -> connected components -> min-area oriented box -> unclip) off the main thread. Index + 4 ladder pages (basics/practical/wild/multi-model incl. DBNet->TrOCR), all verified with real in-browser inference, cached auto-init, clean console, and mobile+desktop parity. Sample images are generated for this demo (no real people/brands).
